content.full_text:
City of Milpitas, California

455 E. Calaveras Boulevard — Milpitas, California 95035  
Telephone: (408) 262-2310

January 19, 1979

FROM THE OFFICE OF: City Clerk

FILE REF:

Fair Political Practices Commission  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division  
1100 K Street  
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Dear Sirs:

One of our Councilmembers, who is forming a committee to seek reelection, has requested information concerning interest that might be earned on campaign contributions placed on deposit either by himself or by his committee.

Would such interest be reported on Forms 420, 430 or 490 as miscellaneous receipts, line 3 of summary page but not reported on Schedule A as directed by instructions on page 21 of the 1978 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act? Could such accrued interest be expended in the campaign?

Sincerely yours,

W. E. Smith  
City Clerk

W. E. Smith—Advice provided by phone that interest on deposited campaign funds is a miscellaneous receipt reportable on line 3 of the summary page but not reportable on Schedule A. Also, advice provided that such interest may be expended in the campaign.
